title: Citrus and vanilla reed diffuser
subtitle: A 100 ml reed diffuser with sweet orange, vanilla oleoresin, and isopropyl alcohol for room fragrance
excerpt: A reed diffuser works by capillary action: the porous reeds draw the fragrance liquid up from the bottle and evaporate it into the room. Isopropyl alcohol thins the carrier oil, increasing the rate at which it travels up the reed and improving the scent throw. Flip the reeds every few days.
sourceNotes: Reed diffuser formulation from standard fragrance and home perfumery guides.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Scent throw develops fully after 24 hours.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 2 to 3 months of active use. The diffuser liquid evaporates over time. Once the reeds are no longer drawing liquid they can be discarded; top up the bottle with fresh liquid. Reeds can be replaced once absorbed.

recipeTools.notes: Rattan reeds are preferred; synthetic reeds draw liquid faster

glossaryTerms.definition: The movement of liquid through narrow channels or porous materials against or in the direction of gravity. In reed diffusers, capillary action draws the fragrance liquid up through the hollow channels in rattan reeds to the top, where it evaporates into the air.

A solvent alcohol used in reed diffusers to thin carrier oils, improving the rate of capillary draw and increasing scent throw. It is optional but typically doubles the effective throw of a reed diffuser.

A concentrated vanilla extract suitable for use in oil-based cosmetic and fragrance products. Unlike vanilla essential oil (which does not exist commercially), oleoresin provides a true warm vanilla scent stable in oil bases.

Turning the diffuser reeds upside down so the dry end is submerged in the liquid and the saturated end is exposed to air. This refreshes the scent throw by exposing fresh, fragrance-laden reed surface.

items.prepNote: 20% of formula; optional but recommended for better throw

content.text: Combine fractionated coconut oil, isopropyl alcohol, sweet orange essential oil, and vanilla oleoresin in a small jug and stir to combine. Pour into the diffuser bottle using a funnel. Insert 6 to 8 rattan reeds. After 1 hour, flip the reeds so the dry ends are submerged. The diffuser will begin to throw scent within a few hours.

Place the diffuser in a well-ventilated spot but not in a direct draught, which causes the liquid to evaporate too quickly. Flip the reeds every 2 to 3 days. The liquid will last 2 to 3 months. Top up with fresh liquid when the level falls below 1 cm, or replace the reeds if they become clogged with dust.
